title: Lists
description: Learn unordered lists, ordered lists, and navigation menu structures.

levels.title: Unordered List
levels.learningPoint: Lists are built with ul > li structure
levels.instructions: Create an unordered list with three items: Apple, Banana, Grape.
levels.hint: ul stands for unordered list, li stands for list item.
levels.successMessage: ul is used when the order of items doesn't matter.

levels.title: Ordered List
levels.learningPoint: Use ol > li when the order matters
levels.instructions: Create an ordered list of cooking steps: Prepare, Cook, Serve.
levels.hint: ol stands for ordered list. Use it when sequence matters.
levels.successMessage: ol is perfect for recipes, instructions, rankings, and any sequential content!

levels.title: Navigation Menu
levels.learningPoint: Navigation menus follow the nav > ul > li > a pattern
levels.instructions: Create a navigation menu with three links: Home, About, Contact.
levels.starterCode:
<nav>
  <!-- Build a ul > li > a structure here -->
</nav>
levels.hint: Put a ul inside nav, then each li contains an a link.
levels.successMessage: nav > ul > li > a is the most common navigation pattern on the web!

levels.title: Nested Lists
levels.learningPoint: You can nest a list inside another list
levels.instructions: Add a sub-list under 'Fruits' with items: Apple, Strawberry.
levels.hint: Put a new ul inside the li to create a sub-list.
levels.successMessage: Nested lists are commonly used for categories. Remember: a new ul goes inside the li!
